full_text:
R2: Make a copy of the template and run the functions from the top button tCycIF MagicGUI
R3: You should give permits for the automation (Google will ask you to authenticate just once)
R4: FOR RESEARCHERS
R5: 📋 Sample_Layout
R6: Panel refers to a specific combination of antibodies used in the full experiment (e.g., A, B, C...).
R7: Versions within each panel indicate the sample split by dilution used (e.g., 1, 2…).
R8: For real experiments (not validations): | → Assign Panel A | → Use Dilution 1 only.
R10: 📋 Panel_Builder
R11: Start by launching the Panel Design Wizard in the t-CyCIF MagicGui.
R12: This links to the most recent version of the Antibody Database
R13: | Use the dropdown or input an antibody ID to auto-fill details.
R14: | Formatting (e.g., colors) will apply a few seconds after selection.
R15: You MUST fill the validation dillution column/s, for version 1 just fill down ValidationDillution1, for v2, ValidationDillution2 ...
R16: | If you are running a real experiment, not a validation,just fill ValidationDillution1 column, you should copy the ValidationDillutionLola for those antibodies that were validated
R17: | ⚠️ IMPORTANT: If an antibody is validated but missing, update the antibody database, not just your local sheet. After updating the database, run the panel design wizard again to link the new version
R18: You MUST fill the cycle number and the panel.

body database, not just your local sheet. After updating the database, run the panel design wizard again to link the new version
R18: You MUST fill the cycle number and the panel.
R19: If an antibody vial is missing you will be notified
R20: 📊 Panel_summary
R21: For presentations, publications, or organization, run 📊 Display Panel_summary in tCyCIF MagicGui.
R22: This will display a new tab per each panel, with format, cycle number, marker number in the final image and all kinds of details
R23: This is useful for later computational steps
R25: FOR RESEARCHERS
R26: 🧪 Pipetting_Calculator
R27: You can adjust per-slide volume and Hoechst dilution for automatic calculations.
R28: 🧪 RunPipettingSteps
R29: If you go to the cycif gui and then runpipettingsteps you will get
R30: 🧪 TubePrepAuto | Antibodies + exact volumes to pipet, organized by vial - set of slides
R31: 🧪 ReagentList | List of required antibodies (calculates total volume needed + locations) | | → Useful for verifying inventory and ordering.
R32: 🧪 TubePrepAuto | By default Slide_Number is displaye in Tube prep auto plan.. To change this setting to Unique_Section_ID, go to extensions->App scripts->tCycIF pipetting plan automation->generatePipettingPlan.gs in row 22 Slide_number to Unique_Section_ID.Remember to click the saving disk on top
R33: 🔬 RunScanningGUIs

App scripts->tCycIF pipetting plan automation->generatePipettingPlan.gs in row 22 Slide_number to Unique_Section_ID.Remember to click the saving disk on top
R33: 🔬 RunScanningGUIs
R34: 🔬 Scanning_Plan | It will display the plan for scanning with slideIDs, running numbers and dropdown menus that autoformat
R35: 🔬 Experimental_Assessment | Automatically displays the sheet
R37: FOR ANALYSES
R38: 💻 Display Computational Steps
R39: Lists the samples and the steps needed in the future
